name: Fluke: PNW Orca Atlas
subtitle: Sightings, whales, and maps
description:
Meet the killer whales of the Pacific Northwest through public records, community observations, and careful context.

SIGHTINGS
Browse sourced public observations across the Salish Sea and nearby waters. Each record keeps its date, location, group size, ecotype, and attribution together. Submit a sighting without creating an account by providing a contact email and at least one photo, or use an optional account; new observations enter public moderation before they appear. If a connection drops, queued uploads remain on your device and retry later.

WHALES
Search a public catalog of individual whales and open profiles with catalog identifiers, pod or ecotype context, and documented relationships when available.

IDENTIFY
Prepare a fluke photo for future matching. Identification is visibly in training and does not invent results while the rights-cleared catalog is being completed.

LEARN
Read field notes about ecotypes, catalog evidence, responsible wildlife viewing, data freshness, and source interpretation.

YOU
Review your local logbook, queued submissions, and optional account.

ATLAS
Open Atlas from Sightings to explore historical ranges, movement traces, timelines, and model estimates. Confidence and source context stay visible so estimates are not presented as certainty.

Fluke is an independent, non-commercial reference project. It is not an emergency alert service and should not be used to approach or pursue wildlife. Always follow current official viewing regulations.
keywords: killer whales,sightings,Salish Sea,catalog,field guide,wildlife,marine biology,Pacific Northwest
promotionalText: Explore sourced PNW orca records, submit moderated sightings, and follow the evidence through a beautifully simple field guide and atlas.
whatsNew: First TestFlight release with five-tab browsing, optional accounts, moderated sighting submissions, queued uploads, and Atlas.
reviewNotes:
Accounts are optional; browsing requires no credentials. A sighting can be submitted without an account by providing a contact email and at least one photo. Signed-in observers can use Sign in with Apple. Submitted observations enter public moderation. Offline submissions and photos use queued uploads and retry when connectivity returns.

The five tabs are Sightings, Whales, Identify, Learn, and You. Atlas opens from the globe button in Sightings. Identify is visibly in training and does not return a match until a rights-cleared catalog is available.

The app uses https://fluke-api.onrender.com. The free-tier service can take up to approximately 60 seconds to wake after inactivity; capability loading retries automatically and browse retry states remain visible.

Suggested review path:
1. Browse Sightings and Whales, then open Atlas from Sightings.
2. Tap Add Sighting, enter an observation and contact email, attach at least one photo, and submit for moderation.
3. Open Identify to verify the training state.
4. Open Learn, then You to inspect the local logbook and optional account.

Camera and Photos access are requested only after the reviewer explicitly chooses those controls. Fluke does not request device location; coarse coordinates are entered with a sighting. It does not use contacts, tracking, or notification permission. Public responses and queued observer content may be stored in the app sandbox. Source attribution remains visible.

Support: https://fluke-pnw.vercel.app/support
Privacy: https://fluke-pnw.vercel.app/privacy
